title: Mixins (Superpowers) 🦸‍♂️
description: Inject standalone abilities perfectly into any class you want.

slides.title: The Inheritance Problem 🧱
slides.content:
# The Single Parent Rule

In Dart, a class can `extend` exactly ONE parent.
What if you have a `class Bird extends Animal` and a `class Airplane extends Vehicle`?

They BOTH need the ability to `fly()`! But you can't put `fly()` in Animal (dogs can't fly) and you can't put it in Vehicle (cars can't fly).
And you definitely shouldn't write `fly()` twice.

slides.title: Enter the Mixin 🧬

quizData.question: Why use a Mixin instead of normal Inheritance (extends)?

options.text: Because classes can only extend from one Parent, but might need isolated abilities shared across different families.

quizData.explanation: Mixins solve the 'multiple inheritance' problem. A Bird and a Plane aren't related, but they share the specific 'Flying' trait.

slides.title: Equipping the Power 🦸

quizData.explanation: The 'with' keyword attaches the Mixin to the Class.

slides.title: See it in Action
slides.content: You can even inject MULTIPLE mixins by separating them with commas: `with Swimmer, Flyer`!
